summary:
Heparin use prior to an oral procedure poses a significant risk due to its effect on extending coagulation pathway inhibition. This increased susceptibility towards protracted bleedings makes it contraindicated unless carefully managed under close monitoring given potential severe complications from even minor intraoral trauma exacerbated when combined chronically over time without sufficient reversal agents available readily post-op if needed emergently as well.
The other medications listed do not have direct effects that would cause uncontrolled bleeding specific enough compared to how critical heparins' mechanism is here making this clearly superior choice
